refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-003 Refined: The NGHE solution shall enable operators and tele-operators to achieve a measured energy efficiency of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target (customer-defined threshold) over the agreed evaluation period, through integrated monitoring, control optimization, and real-time feedback mechanisms.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is outcome-focused but lacks explicit linkage to the mechanisms (monitoring, control, feedback) that enable efficiency achievement. Refinement clarifies that energy efficiency is not a passive property but an active capability requiring measurement, visibility, and adaptive control. This supports both Site Manager acceptance criteria and Operator/Tele-operator decision-making.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure energy_per_ton = (total_energy_consumed_kWh / total_tonnage_moved) across a defined evaluation period (e.g., 1 week, 1 month). Compare against E_target. Verify that the NGHE solution provides real-time telemetry, operator guidance, and control modes that demonstrably reduce energy waste relative to baseline equipment.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh) and work output (tonnage moved) with ≥95% data availability.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time energy efficiency feedback (energy_per_ton KPI) to operators and tele-operators via telemetry interface.
CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall support operator/tele-operator control modes and recommendations that minimize energy waste (e.g., optimal speed profiles, load balancing, idle reduction).
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall enforce safety-critical constraints (from Safety Services) during energy optimization to prevent unsafe efficiency-seeking behavior.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Passive energy monitoring only (measure and log, no active control or feedback)
DP-B: Active energy management system with real-time telemetry feedback, operator guidance, and integrated safety constraints
DP-C: Centralized energy optimization engine with predictive modeling and autonomous control override (high automation, lower operator involvement)
DP-D: Distributed energy monitoring across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules with independent optimization logic (risk of inconsistency)
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Active energy management system with real-time telemetry feedback, operator guidance, and integrated safety constraints. The system shall continuously measure energy and work output, compute energy_per_ton in real-time, communicate KPI and recommendations to operators/tele-operators via Telemetry interface, and enforce safety-critical constraints from Safety Services before executing any control action.

potential_sources: Coupling between energy optimization logic and safety constraint enforcement (mitigated by explicit safety gate in DP)
Coupling between Vehicle control and Teleoperation feedback (mitigated by Telemetry interface as single source of truth)
Coupling between real-time KPI computation and network latency (acceptable for non-safety-critical feedback; safety actions use SafetyAlert)
coupling_check.notes: DP-B avoids the pitfall of DP-C (autonomous override without operator awareness) and DP-D (distributed logic leading to inconsistency). The design maintains clear separation: Vehicle executes control, Teleoperation provides operator interface, Safety Services enforces constraints, and Telemetry carries KPI data. This is a decoupled architecture.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Energy consumption can be measured at Vehicle level with sufficient accuracy (±5%) to compute meaningful KPI.
ASSUMPTION: Operators and tele-operators will act on energy efficiency feedback if it does not conflict with safety or productivity.
RISK: If network latency on Telemetry exceeds 2 seconds, real-time feedback may be perceived as stale; mitigation: buffer local KPI computation at Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

RISK: Safety constraints may conflict with energy optimization (e.g., reduced speed for safety vs. optimal speed for efficiency); mitigation: explicit priority rule in DP (safety always overrides efficiency).
RISK: Operator behavior change is not guaranteed; energy efficiency depends on human factors beyond system design.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify in DP-B the priority rule: 'Safety constraints from Safety Services are evaluated first; energy optimization recommendations are issued only if safety is maintained.' Add explicit statement: 'The system shall NOT autonomously override operator control for energy optimization; recommendations are advisory only.'

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. It owns the energy management logic, real-time KPI computation, and coordination of feedback and constraints across sub-modules. The module is responsible for ensuring that energy_per_ton measurement and optimization occur consistently across all operational modes (manual, tele-operated, autonomous).

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution outputs real-time energy_per_ton KPI, energy consumption (kWh), tonnage moved, and operator recommendations to Site Manager, Equipment Operator, and Tele-operator dashboards.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives work tasks (e.g., 'move X tons from location A to B') from Site Manager or autonomous planner; energy optimization is scoped within task constraints.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety constraints and alerts from Safety Services; energy optimization is gated by safety status.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link to synchronize Telemetry, Tasking, and SafetyAlert across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of energy management logic and KPI computation. All sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) contribute data and constraints, but the orchestration and consistency of energy efficiency measurement and optimization is a system-level concern. Minimal diffusion: only the Telemetry interface is shared externally.

Vehicle could own energy measurement and local optimization (e.g., engine efficiency, idle reduction), but energy_per_ton KPI requires work output data that may not be available at Vehicle level alone. Assigning energy management to Vehicle alone would diffuse the KPI computation across Vehicle and Teleoperation, risking inconsistency.

Teleoperation could own operator feedback and recommendations, but energy measurement and KPI computation require Vehicle-level data. Assigning energy management to Teleoperation alone would create a dependency on Vehicle data without clear ownership of the KPI logic.

Safety Services owns constraint enforcement, not energy optimization. Assigning energy management to Safety Services would conflate two distinct concerns and create high diffusion of energy logic across the system.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate owner of CR-NGHE-003 because: (1) energy_per_ton is a system-level KPI that requires integration of Vehicle energy data and work output data; (2) energy optimization must be coordinated with safety constraints (Safety Services) and operator feedback (Teleoperation); (3) centralizing energy management logic in NGHE Solution avoids diffusion and ensures consistency across all operational modes. Vehicle and Teleoperation are sub-modules that contribute data and constraints but do not own the energy management logic.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution becomes a 'god module' that owns too many concerns (energy, safety, teleoperation, vehicle control), it may become difficult to test and maintain. MITIGATION: Ensure NGHE Solution is a thin orchestration layer that delegates specific concerns to sub-modules (e.g., Vehicle owns engine efficiency, Safety Services owns constraint enforcement) and coordinates via well-defined interfaces.

RISK: If energy measurement is not synchronized across Vehicle and Teleoperation, KPI computation may be inconsistent. MITIGATION: Establish a single source of truth for energy and work output data (e.g., Vehicle publishes via Telemetry, NGHE Solution computes KPI, Teleoperation displays KPI).

RISK: If Telemetry interface is not reliable, operators may not receive timely energy feedback. MITIGATION: Implement local KPI buffering at Vehicle and Teleoperation; use Network Link redundancy if available.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify the NGHE Solution module scope: 'NGHE Solution owns the energy management logic, real-time KPI computation (energy_per_ton), and coordination of energy optimization with safety constraints. Vehicle owns energy measurement and local efficiency controls (e.g., engine tuning). Teleoperation owns operator feedback and recommendations. Safety Services owns constraint enforcement. All modules communicate via Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link interfaces.' This explicit delegation prevents diffusion and clarifies ownership boundaries.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-B (Active energy management system with real-time telemetry feedback and integrated safety constraints) directly addresses the refined FR (enable operators to achieve energy_per_ton ≤ E_target through monitoring, control optimization, and feedback). The DP specifies the mechanism (real-time KPI computation, operator guidance, safety gating) that enables the FR outcome. No decoupling or missing logic detected.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK: If energy measurement logic is duplicated in both Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, KPI computation may diverge. MITIGATION: Establish Vehicle as the single source of truth for energy data; Teleoperation receives energy data via Telemetry and computes KPI locally for display only (read-only copy).

DIFFUSION RISK: If safety constraint enforcement is split between Safety Services and NGHE Solution, energy optimization may bypass safety checks. MITIGATION: Enforce a strict rule that NGHE Solution queries Safety Services for constraint status before issuing optimization recommendations.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner of energy management logic. The module boundary is clear: NGHE Solution orchestrates energy measurement, KPI computation, and safety gating. Vehicle and Teleoperation are sub-modules that contribute data and constraints via well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert). No significant containment issues detected.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-003 is feasible: (1) Measure energy_per_ton over an agreed evaluation period (e.g., 1 week). (2) Compare against E_target. (3) Verify that NGHE Solution provides real-time telemetry, operator guidance, and safety gating. (4) Conduct operator acceptance testing to confirm that feedback is actionable and leads to efficiency improvements. Verification is testable and measurable.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of CR-NGHE-003 to NGHE Solution is clear. Sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-003.1 through CR-NGHE-003.4) are allocated to specific sub-modules: CR-NGHE-003.1 (measurement) to Vehicle; CR-NGHE-003.2 (feedback) to Teleoperation; CR-NGHE-003.3 (control modes) to Vehicle and Teleoperation; CR-NGHE-003.4 (safety constraints) to Safety Services. Each sub-FR has a clear owner and verification method.

recommended_revisions: Revise DP-B to explicitly state the priority rule: 'Safety constraints from Safety Services are evaluated first; energy optimization recommendations are issued only if safety is maintained. The system shall NOT autonomously override operator control for energy optimization.'

Revise NGHE Solution module scope to clarify delegation: 'NGHE Solution owns energy management logic and KPI computation. Vehicle owns energy measurement and local efficiency controls. Teleoperation owns operator feedback. Safety Services owns constraint enforcement.'
Add a sub-FR for energy measurement accuracy: 'CR-NGHE-003.1.1: Energy consumption and work output shall be measured with ±5% accuracy to ensure reliable KPI computation.'
Add a sub-FR for operator behavior: 'CR-NGHE-003.5: The NGHE solution shall provide operator training and incentives to encourage adoption of energy-efficient practices based on real-time feedback.'

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-003 is not atomic and requires further decomposition. The refined FR identifies four sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-003.1 through CR-NGHE-003.4) that address distinct concerns: measurement, feedback, control modes, and safety gating. Each sub-FR requires its own FR-DP-IM decomposition to ensure that the energy management system is fully specified and allocated. The current level (system-level energy efficiency KPI) is appropriate for NGHE Solution ownership, but the sub-FRs must be decomposed to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules to clarify implementation details and ownership boundaries.
decision.justification_detail: The FR-DP-IM gates all pass, indicating that the current level is logically sound and architecturally feasible. However, the sub-FRs are not yet decomposed, and their allocation to sub-modules is not yet verified. Proceeding to the next level will ensure that: (1) energy measurement logic is clearly owned by Vehicle; (2) operator feedback and recommendations are clearly owned by Teleoperation; (3) safety constraint enforcement is clearly owned by Safety Services; (4) the interfaces between modules (Telemetry, SafetyAlert) are fully specified. This decomposition is necessary to avoid diffusion and ensure that the energy management system is implementable and testable.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh) and work output (tonnage moved) with ≥95% data availability.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time energy efficiency feedback (energy_per_ton KPI) to operators and tele-operators via telemetry interface.
CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall support operator/tele-operator control modes and recommendations that minimize energy waste (e.g., optimal speed profiles, load balancing, idle reduction).
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall enforce safety-critical constraints (from Safety Services) during energy optimization to prevent unsafe efficiency-seeking behavior.

assumptions: Energy consumption can be measured at Vehicle level with ±5% accuracy.
Operators and tele-operators will act on energy efficiency feedback if it does not conflict with safety or productivity.
Network latency on Telemetry is <2 seconds for real-time feedback to be perceived as actionable.
Safety constraints from Safety Services are available and can be queried by NGHE Solution in real-time.
E_target is defined by the customer and is achievable with the NGHE solution (not a constraint on the design itself).
Operator behavior change is a success factor but not guaranteed by system design alone.
open_risks: RISK: Network latency may exceed 2 seconds, making real-time feedback stale. MITIGATION: Implement local KPI buffering at Vehicle and Teleoperation.
RISK: Safety constraints may conflict with energy optimization. MITIGATION: Establish explicit priority rule (safety always overrides efficiency).
RISK: Operator behavior may not change despite feedback. MITIGATION: Conduct operator training and incentive programs.
RISK: Energy measurement accuracy may be insufficient (>5% error). MITIGATION: Validate measurement hardware and calibration procedures.
RISK: Diffusion of energy management logic across modules may lead to inconsistency. MITIGATION: Centralize KPI computation in NGHE Solution; delegate measurement and control to sub-modules.
evidence_needed: Measurement hardware specifications and calibration procedures for energy consumption and work output sensors.
Network latency and reliability specifications for Telemetry interface.
Safety constraint definitions and query interface from Safety Services.
Operator feedback and acceptance criteria for energy efficiency recommendations.
Baseline energy efficiency data from existing equipment to establish E_target and validate improvement.
Operator training materials and incentive program design to encourage adoption of energy-efficient practices.
